questions.question: A music track has a rhythm pattern of $3:4$ between the guitar and drums. If the guitar plays $12$ notes, how many notes will the drums play?
An artist mixes paints in a ratio of $2:5$ for a specific shade. If she uses $10$ mL of the first paint, how much of the second paint should she use?
A sound wave has a frequency ratio of $1:2$ between two notes. If the first note has a frequency of $20$ Hz, what is the frequency of the second note?
A canvas has a width to length ratio of $3:5$. If the width is $9$ inches, what is the length of the canvas?
A musician plays a rhythm with a $2:3$ ratio between quarter notes and eighth notes. If she plays $8$ quarter notes, how many eighth notes will she play?
An art piece has a color ratio of $4:7$ between two shades. If $16$ parts of the first shade are used, how many parts of the second shade should be used?
A sound system has a volume ratio of $2:3$ between two speakers. If the first speaker is set to $6$, what should the second speaker be set to?
A painter uses a $3:2$ ratio of paint to thinner. If she uses $6$ mL of paint, how much thinner should she use?

A musician is creating a song with a ratio of $5:6$ between the duration of the intro and the outro. If the intro lasts $15$ seconds, how many seconds will the outro last? Show your work and explain your reasoning.

An artist is designing a mural with a ratio of $4:9$ between the width and length. If the width is $12$ feet, what will be the length of the mural? Use cross multiplication to solve the proportion and explain your answer.
